passage:
In the 2006 film version of *The Devil Wears Prada*, Miranda
Priestly, played by Meryl Streep, scolds her unattractive assistant for
imagining that high fashion doesn't affect her. Priestly explains how
the deep blue color of the assistant's sweater descended over the years
from fashion shows to department stores and to the bargain bin in which
the poor girl doubtless found her garment.

This top-down conception of the fashion business couldn't be more out of
date or at odds with the feverish world described
in *Overdressed,* Elizabeth Cline's
three-year indictment of "fast fashion". In the last
decade or so, advances in technology have allowed mass-market labels
such as Zara, H
| M, and Uniqlo to react to trends more quickly and
anticipate demand more precisely. Quicker turnarounds mean less wasted
inventory, more frequent releases, and more profit. These labels
encourage style-conscious consumers to see clothes as disposable - meant
to last only a wash or two, although they don't advertise that - and to
renew their wardrobe every few weeks. By offering on-trend items at
dirt-cheap prices, Cline argues, these brands have hijacked fashion
cycles, shaking an industry long accustomed to a seasonal pace.

The victims of this revolution, of course, are not limited to designers.
For H
| M to offer a
$5.95 knit miniskirt in all its 2, 300-plus stores
around the world, it must rely on low-wage overseas labor, order in
volumes that strain natural resources, and use massive amounts of
harmful chemicals.

*Overdressed* is the fashion world's answer to consumer-activist
bestsellers like Michael Pollan's *The Omnivore's Dilemma*.
"Mass-produced clothing, like fast food, fills a hunger and need, yet
is non-durable and wasteful," Cline argues. Americans, she finds, buy
roughly 20 billion garments a year - about 64 items per person - and no
matter how much they give away, this excess leads to waste.

Towards the end of *Overdressed*, Cline introduced her ideal, a
Brooklyn woman named Sarah Kate Beaumont, who since 2008 has made all of
her own clothes - and beautifully. But as Cline is the first to note, it
took Beaumont decades to perfect her craft; her example can't be knocked
off.

Though several fast-fashion companies have made efforts to curb their
impact on labor and the environment - including H
| M, with its green
Conscious Collection line - Cline believes lasting change can only be
effected by the customer. She exhibits the idealism common to many
advocates of sustainability, be it in food or in energy. Vanity is a
constant; people will only start shopping more sustainably when they
can't afford not to.

questions.questionText: Priestly criticizes her assistant for her ____.
questions.question: Priestly criticizes her assistant for her ____.
questions.explanation: 定位首段：Priestly scolds her assistant for imagining that high fashion doesn't affect her，说明她批评助理对时尚影响不敏感，故选 B。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 利用了 bargain bin 干扰，但原文不是说她讨价还价能力差；D 利用 imagining 干扰，但重点不是缺乏想象力。
questions.commonMistake: 容易看到 bargain bin 或 imagining 就机械对应选项，忽略 scolds ... for 后面的真正原因。
questions.location: P1: Priestly scolds her assistant for imagining that high fashion doesn't affect her.
questions.thinkingMethod: 看到 criticize/scold sb. for... 题干，优先定位 for 后面的原因，再找选项同义替换。

questions.questionText: According to Cline, mass-market labels urge consumers to ____.
questions.question: According to Cline, mass-market labels urge consumers to ____.

options.B: shut out the feverish fashion world
options.D: shop for their garments more frequently

questions.explanation: 定位第二段：这些品牌让消费者把衣服看成一次性物品，并 every few weeks 更新衣橱，对应 D：更频繁地购买服装。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 与原文方向相反，快时尚导致浪费而非鼓励抵制浪费；B、C 原文无对应。
questions.commonMistake: 容易被 waste、advertise 等词吸引，忽略 urge consumers to 后面真正对应的是 renew their wardrobe every few weeks。
questions.location: P2: These labels encourage style-conscious consumers to see clothes as disposable ... and to renew their wardrobe every few weeks.
questions.thinkingMethod: 细节题先找题干关键词 mass-market labels / consumers，再核对动词 encourage 的宾语内容。

questions.questionText: The word "indictment" (Line 3, Para. 2) is closest in meaning to ____.
questions.question: The word "indictment" (Line 3, Para. 2) is closest in meaning to ____.
questions.explanation: indictment 在这里修饰 Elizabeth Cline 对 fast fashion 的三年研究，后文多处说明其批判快时尚的危害，因此接近 accusation。
questions.trapAnalysis: B、D 是正向态度，C 是无所谓态度，都与全文批判语气不符。
questions.commonMistake: 容易只看生词本身，不结合后文 hijacked、victims、wasteful 等负向词判断态度。
questions.location: P2: Elizabeth Cline's three-year indictment of "fast fashion".
questions.thinkingMethod: 词义题回到所在句和后文情感色彩，用上下文态度判断语境义。

questions.questionText: Which of the following can be inferred from the last paragraph?
questions.question: Which of the following can be inferred from the last paragraph?

options.A: Vanity has more often been found in idealists.
options.C: People are more interested in unaffordable garments.
options.D: Pricing is vital to environment-friendly purchasing.

questions.explanation: 末段说 lasting change can only be effected by the customer，并以 can't afford not to 收束，说明价格因素会影响消费者是否选择更可持续的购买方式，故选 D。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 误读 Vanity is a constant；B 与 several fast-fashion companies have made efforts 相反；C 原文没有说消费者更喜欢买买不起的衣服。
questions.commonMistake: 容易把 vanity 单词单独拿出来过度推理，没抓住 can't afford not to 与 sustainable shopping 的关系。
questions.location: P6: people will only start shopping more sustainably when they can't afford not to.
questions.thinkingMethod: 推理题要把末句关键词 afford 与 sustainably 联系起来，不要脱离原文扩展。

questions.questionText: What is the subject of the text?
questions.question: What is the subject of the text?
questions.explanation: 全文从电影引入，核心转向 Elizabeth Cline 对 fast fashion 的批判：低价、频繁更新、浪费、劳工和环境影响，因此主题是对快时尚产业的批评。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 只抓生活方式；B 只对应开头高端时尚神话；D 过窄，文章不是揭秘某个秘密，而是批判一个产业模式。
questions.commonMistake: 容易被第一段电影例子带偏，把引子当主题。
questions.location: 全文主旨，重点看 P2-P6 对 fast fashion 的批判。
questions.thinkingMethod: 主旨题不要停在开头例子，要看全文反复出现和展开论证的核心对象。

articleStructure: 电影例子引入高端时尚影响 → 转入快时尚改变行业节奏 → 分析劳工、资源和浪费问题 → 提出消费者端改变的可能。
notes: 2013 Text 1 已核对公开答案来源并修正 21-25 答案：B D A D C。

sources.name: 中国教育在线 2013 英语一真题答案
新东方在线 2013 英语一答案详解 PDF
新浪教育 2013 英语一阅读答案
